project.name: ruvector slice 2 — RuVector Shared Brain stack
project.description: Slice 2 of RuVector covering the Shared Brain MCP stack: mcp-brain (stdio MCP server library and binary for share, search, vote, transfer learning, Brainpedia pages, and WASM executable nodes over JSON-RPC), mcp-brain-server (Cloud Run backend on axum REST with Firestore + GCS storage, SONA learning, GWT attention, temporal delta tracking, meta-learning exploration, RVF AGI container stack, and Midstream real-time analysis), and mcp-gate (MCP server for the Anytime-Valid Coherence Gate built on cognitum-gate-tilezero).

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the mcp-brain crate — the client-side MCP server library that exposes the brain_* tools (share, search, transfer learning) to Claude Code sessions. Declares dependencies on rmcp, reqwest, ed25519-dalek, and the RuVector core/RVF crates.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for mcp-brain that wires up the client, embed, pipeline, server, tools, and types modules and re-exports McpBrainServer as the public entry point. Documents the ten brain_* MCP tools the crate provides to Claude Code sessions.

nodes.name: tools.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the McpBrainTools dispatcher that lists and routes every brain_* MCP tool call (share, search, vote, transfer, drift, partition, list, delete, status, sync, plus page/node lifecycle) to the Cloud Run backend at pi.ruv.io. Defines the BrainError taxonomy used across the client tool surface.
nodes.languageNotes: Centralizes all 27 brain_* tool handlers behind a single async dispatcher with structured JSON-RPC-style request/response shapes.

nodes.name: BrainError
nodes.summary: Error enum covering network, serialization, backend, and validation failures encountered while invoking remote brain tools.

nodes.name: McpBrainTools
nodes.summary: Stateful MCP tool holder that owns the HTTP client and backend URL, exposing list_tools, call_tool, and one async method per brain_* operation.

nodes.name: list_tools
nodes.summary: Returns the JSON-Schema-described catalog of every brain_* MCP tool that this server exposes to Claude Code clients.

nodes.name: call_tool
nodes.summary: Top-level dispatcher that matches an incoming MCP tool name and arguments to the appropriate brain_* handler method.

nodes.name: brain_share
nodes.summary: Posts a new shared learning (with optional embeddings, tags, and witness metadata) to the Cloud Run backend's /share endpoint.

nodes.name: brain_search
nodes.summary: Semantic-plus-keyword search across shared knowledge by forwarding the query to the backend search endpoint.

nodes.name: brain_sync
nodes.summary: Performs bidirectional sync of local versus remote memories, reconciling vector clocks and reputation deltas.

nodes.name: brain_page_create
nodes.summary: Creates a Wikipedia-style collaborative page on the shared brain for an evolving topic.

nodes.name: brain_node_wasm
nodes.summary: Fetches the WASM artifact metadata for a published cognitive node so clients can sandbox-execute it.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build that compiles the mcp-brain-server (axum REST API + Firestore + GCS) on Rust nightly-2026-03-20 and ships a slim Debian runtime image listening on port 8080 — the image deployed to Cloud Run at pi.ruv.io.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-stage build separates the rustlang/rust:nightly builder from the debian:bookworm-slim runtime to keep the deployed image small.

nodes.name: Dockerfile.worker
nodes.summary: Builds the worker-flavored mcp-brain-server image used for background ingestion, training, and PubMed discovery jobs that run outside the main Cloud Run API service.

nodes.name: monitoring-dashboard.json
nodes.summary: GCP Cloud Monitoring dashboard definition (mosaicLayout) tracking request volume, latency, and error rate of the ruvbrain Cloud Run service.

nodes.name: cloudbuild-trainer.yaml
nodes.summary: Cloud Build pipeline that produces the gcr.io/$PROJECT_ID/ruvbrain-trainer image used by the scheduled training jobs feeding the shared brain.

nodes.name: pubmed_discover.sh
nodes.summary: Bash discovery script that queries PubMed for new biomedical literature and pushes candidate papers into the brain's ingest pipeline for embedding and storage.

nodes.name: ruvbrain_sse.rs
nodes.summary: Standalone SSE (Server-Sent Events) binary that wraps the brain server with an MCP-over-HTTP transport, exposing /health, /sse, and /messages routes for streaming agent sessions.

nodes.name: sse_handler
nodes.summary: Long-lived SSE handler that registers a session and streams MCP JSON-RPC events back to the connected agent client.

nodes.name: messages_handler
nodes.summary: Receives client-to-server MCP messages over POST and forwards them into the active SSE session channel.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Boots the SSE server: loads env config, builds the axum router with shared AppState, and binds to the listening port for Cloud Run.

nodes.name: shutdown_signal
nodes.summary: Awaits SIGINT/SIGTERM and triggers a graceful axum shutdown so in-flight SSE sessions close cleanly.

nodes.name: embeddings.rs
nodes.summary: EmbeddingEngine wrapper that turns text into dense vectors using either an on-device model or a TF-IDF/RLM hash fallback, used by every brain ingest and search path.

nodes.name: EmbeddingEngine
nodes.summary: Encapsulates the embedding backend (corpus, RLM state, dim, engine name) and exposes embed and embed_for_storage entry points used by store and routes.

nodes.name: new
nodes.summary: Initializes the embedding engine, choosing the active backend based on env config and seeding the local corpus.

nodes.name: embed
nodes.summary: Produces a query-time embedding for a single input string.

nodes.name: embed_for_storage
nodes.summary: Produces a storage-time embedding (potentially with quantization or different normalization than query embeddings).

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for mcp-brain-server that exposes every backend submodule (storage, embeddings, routes, optimizer, web memory, trainer, etc.) consumed by the axum REST API and the SSE binary.

nodes.name: optimizer.rs
nodes.summary: GeminiOptimizer module that periodically consults Google Gemini to suggest improvements to stored propositions — proposal generation, scoring, and metrics emission for the shared brain.

nodes.name: GeminiOptimizer
nodes.summary: Background optimizer that batches proposition samples, calls Gemini, and writes back optimization suggestions, throttled by an is_due schedule.

nodes.name: optimize
nodes.summary: Executes one optimization pass: builds the prompt, calls Gemini, parses suggestions, and updates metrics.

nodes.name: build_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the Gemini prompt by embedding sampled propositions, context, and the desired suggestion schema.

nodes.name: call_gemini
nodes.summary: Issues the HTTP request to the Gemini API, handling auth, retries, and response decoding.

nodes.name: parse_suggestions
nodes.summary: Validates and decodes Gemini's JSON response into structured OptimizationSuggestion records.

nodes.name: ranking.rs
nodes.summary: Tiny RankingEngine that scores and orders search hits returned by store keyword/vector search before they hit the REST response.

nodes.name: RankingEngine
nodes.summary: Holds ranking weights and exposes a rank method that sorts memories by combined relevance, quality, and recency.

nodes.name: store.rs
nodes.summary: Core Firestore/GCS storage primitives for the brain server — FirestoreClient handles auth, put/delete/list, memory CRUD, vote tracking, reputation, page/delta lifecycle, and node publish/revoke. The single biggest module in the backend.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines low-level Google REST API calls with high-level domain operations (memory, page, node, reputation) behind one FirestoreClient facade.

nodes.name: FirestoreClient
nodes.summary: Authenticated client wrapping reqwest with Google token refresh, an in-memory token cache, and 50+ methods covering every persistence operation in the brain server.

nodes.name: StoreError
nodes.summary: Error enum covering Firestore/GCS/auth/serde failures surfaced by the storage layer.

nodes.name: get_token
nodes.summary: Returns a cached Google OAuth access token or triggers a refresh when expired.

nodes.name: refresh_token
nodes.summary: Exchanges the service-account JWT for a fresh OAuth access token against Google's token endpoint.

nodes.name: firestore_put
nodes.summary: Writes a Firestore document with the supplied path and JSON body, encoding values into Firestore's type-tagged format.

nodes.name: firestore_list
nodes.summary: Paginated Firestore listing with filters/order/limit and decoding back into native Rust types — the longest method in the module.

nodes.name: load_from_firestore
nodes.summary: Bulk-loads memories from Firestore into the in-process index on startup.

nodes.name: store_memory
nodes.summary: Persists a BrainMemory record to Firestore and updates the local index.

nodes.name: search_memories
nodes.summary: Top-level memory search that combines vector similarity with keyword filtering.

nodes.name: keyword_search
nodes.summary: Token-overlap keyword search across stored memories, used both as fallback and as a hybrid component of search_memories.

nodes.name: update_quality
nodes.summary: Applies a vote to a memory, updating Bayesian quality score, reputation, and poisoning checks atomically.

nodes.name: check_poisoning
nodes.summary: Detects coordinated low-quality submissions and flags them for review or removal.

nodes.name: create_page
nodes.summary: Creates a new collaborative page record (Wikipedia-style topic) with its initial revision.

nodes.name: promote_page
nodes.summary: Promotes an accumulated page draft into a canonical published revision after delta/evidence checks.

nodes.name: publish_node
nodes.summary: Publishes a cognitive node (WASM artifact + manifest) into the federated node registry.

nodes.name: revoke_node
nodes.summary: Revokes a previously published node, removing it from the registry and notifying subscribers.

nodes.name: types.rs
nodes.summary: Central type catalog for the brain server: ~73 structs/enums covering AppState, BrainMemory, request/response shapes for every REST endpoint, page/node lifecycle types, LoRA federation store, RVF feature flags, and evidence/voting models.
nodes.languageNotes: Acts as the schema contract between routes.rs, store.rs, and external API consumers — almost every other backend module imports from here.

nodes.name: AppState
nodes.summary: Aggregate axum application state shared across handlers: FirestoreClient, embedding engine, ranking, optimizer, pipeline, rate limiter, and feature flags.

nodes.name: BrainCategory
nodes.summary: Taxonomy enum for memory categories with display/parse helpers.

nodes.name: BrainMemory
nodes.summary: Canonical record for a shared learning: content, embedding, signature, category, reputation, witness chain.

nodes.name: ShareRequest
nodes.summary: Request body for /share — content plus signing/witness metadata posted by the brain_share MCP tool.

nodes.name: StatusResponse
nodes.summary: Response shape for the /status endpoint, summarizing memory counts, queue depth, embedding state, and feature flags.

nodes.name: EvidenceType
nodes.summary: Enum classifying evidence attached to page deltas (citation, replication, contradiction, audit).

nodes.name: CreatePageRequest
nodes.summary: Request body for creating a new collaborative page with title, category, and initial content.

nodes.name: WasmNode
nodes.summary: Manifest for a published WASM cognitive node — hash, bytes URL, capability set, signature.

nodes.name: LoraFederationStore
nodes.summary: Stores federated LoRA adapter weights with eight operations for upload, merge, list, fetch, and aggregation across contributors.

nodes.name: RvfFeatureFlags
nodes.summary: Toggleable RVF feature switches (witness chains, DP proofs, mincut partitioning, training jobs) read from env at boot.

nodes.name: PipelineState
nodes.summary: Runtime state of the ingest/training pipeline with two helper methods.

nodes.name: web_memory.rs
nodes.summary: Web-memory subsystem: ingests crawled web pages, tiered-compresses them (CompressionTier), tracks link edges and content deltas, and answers contradiction/novelty/evolution queries against the web knowledge graph.

nodes.name: WebMemory
nodes.summary: Top-level container for a cleaned, embedded, classified web page with revisions and summary helper.

nodes.name: CompressionTier
nodes.summary: Enum representing the compression tier applied to a stored web page (raw, summarized, gisted, vectored).

nodes.name: WebPageDelta
nodes.summary: Diff record describing how a web page changed between two captures, used for evolution queries.

nodes.name: ContentDelta
nodes.summary: Fine-grained content delta with classify helper to label change kinds (additive, contradictory, structural).

nodes.name: LinkEdge
nodes.summary: Edge in the web link graph with type (citation, follow, mention) and weight.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Browser-facing dashboard for the brain server with inline JS/CSS — surfaces memory feed, voting UI, web ingest views, and admin controls against the same axum REST API used by MCP clients.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-file SPA — no separate JS bundle; the entire UI is bundled into one HTML document served directly by axum's static-file route.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the mcp-gate crate — the Anytime-Valid Coherence Gate MCP server that decides permit/defer/deny on agent actions before they execute.

nodes.name: tools.rs
nodes.summary: McpGateTools dispatcher implementing the Anytime-Valid Coherence Gate — exposes permit_action, get_receipt, and replay_decision MCP tools plus internal helpers that build witness info and explain defer/deny reasons.

nodes.name: McpError
nodes.summary: Error enum for the coherence gate covering invalid arguments, internal failures, and replay/cache misses.

nodes.name: McpGateTools
nodes.summary: MCP tool holder for the Coherence Gate exposing permit_action/get_receipt/replay_decision and the witness/defer/deny helper methods.

nodes.name: list_tools
nodes.summary: Returns the JSON-Schema catalog for the three gate tools (permit_action, get_receipt, replay_decision).

nodes.name: call_tool
nodes.summary: Dispatches an incoming MCP tool name to permit_action, get_receipt, or replay_decision.

nodes.name: permit_action
nodes.summary: Core gate decision: evaluates an action against coherence-gate evidence and returns permit, defer (with reason), or deny (with reason) plus a signed receipt.

nodes.name: get_receipt
nodes.summary: Fetches a previously issued decision receipt by id for audit replay.

nodes.name: replay_decision
nodes.summary: Re-runs a stored decision against the current rule set to detect drift between old and new policy outcomes.

nodes.name: build_witness_info
nodes.summary: Assembles the witness chain (signatures, evidence ids, timestamp) attached to a gate decision receipt.

nodes.name: determine_defer_reason
nodes.summary: Inspects evidence to choose a human-readable defer reason (e.g., insufficient consensus, awaiting witness).

nodes.name: determine_deny_reason
nodes.summary: Inspects evidence to choose a human-readable deny reason (e.g., policy violation, blocklist match, replay anomaly).

nodes.name: README.md

nodes.name: scheduler-jobs.yaml
nodes.summary: Cloud Scheduler job definitions that periodically invoke training, drift_check, transfer_all, rebuild_graph, cleanup, and attractor_analysis worker actions on the brain.

nodes.name: cloudbuild-worker.yaml

nodes.name: aggregate.rs
nodes.summary: Byzantine-fault-tolerant aggregator that combines contributor embeddings while bounding the influence of outliers — used when merging brain_share contributions.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements Krum/median-based aggregation guarded against adversarial contributors.

nodes.name: ByzantineAggregator
nodes.summary: Aggregator that fuses many contributor embeddings into a single centroid while penalizing outliers — a Byzantine-fault-tolerant federation primitive.

nodes.name: aggregate
nodes.summary: Robust mean of embedding contributions with outlier trimming based on distance to provisional centroid.

nodes.name: aggregate_weighted
nodes.summary: Weighted aggregation variant taking per-contributor confidence scores into account when fusing embeddings.

nodes.name: count_outliers
nodes.summary: Computes how many embeddings deviate beyond an outlier threshold — exposed for the brain's federation metrics.

nodes.name: ruvbrain_worker.rs
nodes.summary: Batch worker binary for Cloud Run Jobs that runs scheduler actions (train, drift_check, transfer_all, rebuild_graph, cleanup, attractor_analysis) as one-shot CLI invocations using the shared AppState.
nodes.languageNotes: Reuses the AppState built by routes::create_router for code reuse, then discards the Router.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Async tokio main: parses WORKER_ACTIONS env var, builds AppState, dispatches each action sequentially, and exits non-zero if any action fails.

nodes.name: run_action

nodes.name: gcs.rs
nodes.summary: Google Cloud Storage client used by the brain to persist RVF artifacts: handles OAuth token refresh via the metadata server and supports upload/download/delete by contributor and memory id.

nodes.name: GcsError
nodes.summary: Error variants produced by the GCS client (HTTP failures, auth failures, missing objects).

nodes.name: GcsClient
nodes.summary: Cached GCS client for RVF artifact storage — handles metadata-server OAuth, token refresh, and bucket-scoped CRUD on RVF objects.

nodes.name: upload_rvf
nodes.summary: Uploads an RVF artifact to GCS under contributor/memory_id with appropriate content type and OAuth bearer auth.

nodes.name: download_rvf
nodes.summary: Downloads an RVF artifact by contributor and memory id, returning bytes or a structured GcsError.

nodes.name: refresh_token
nodes.summary: Refreshes the GCS OAuth token using the GCE metadata server, with caching to avoid stampeding the metadata endpoint.

nodes.name: main.rs
nodes.summary: Boot entry point of the mcp-brain-server: builds the axum router, starts the listener immediately on PORT, then spawns the cognitive training/tick loop and graceful-shutdown handlers in the background.
nodes.languageNotes: Server starts immediately so health/ready endpoints respond during background Firestore hydration — important for Cloud Run.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Async tokio main that boots the axum server, schedules the 5-minute enhanced training cycle and the 60-second cognitive tick (GWT broadcast + curiosity).

nodes.name: shutdown_signal
nodes.summary: Listens for SIGINT and SIGTERM and returns when either fires, enabling graceful axum shutdown on Cloud Run signals.

nodes.name: pipeline.rs
nodes.summary: Massive ingest/training pipeline for the brain: builds RVF containers, ingests from Pub/Sub + RSS/Atom feeds + CommonCrawl/CDX, deduplicates content, runs the optimization scheduler, and collects pipeline metrics.
nodes.languageNotes: 1700+ line module containing the RVF builder, Pub/Sub push consumer, dedup, optimization scheduler, feed ingester and Common Crawl/CDX adapter — the heart of brain ingestion.

nodes.name: RvfPipelineInput
nodes.summary: Input descriptor for assembling an RVF container — title, content, embedding, tags, contributor signing key references.

nodes.name: build_rvf_container
nodes.summary: Assembles an RVF container from a pipeline input — packs title, content, embedding, and metadata into the binary RVF format.

nodes.name: PubSubClient
nodes.summary: Pub/Sub push client that decodes envelopes from the GCP Pub/Sub push subscription and acks processed messages with OAuth bearer auth.

nodes.name: DataInjector
nodes.summary: Stateful data injector that processes incoming injection items, content-hash deduplicates, and counts new items for the training loop.

nodes.name: OptimizationScheduler
nodes.summary: Scheduler that decides when to run optimization tasks (retrain, drift_check, etc.) based on new-item thresholds and cycle counters.

nodes.name: MetricsCollector
nodes.summary: Runtime metrics aggregator tracking received/processed/failed items, injections-per-minute, and queue depth for the pipeline.

nodes.name: FeedIngester
nodes.summary: RSS/Atom feed ingester that fetches due feeds, parses XML, deduplicates entries, and emits InjectionItems into the training pipeline.

nodes.name: CommonCrawlAdapter
nodes.summary: Common Crawl / CDX adapter that queries the CDX API, fetches WARC pages, extracts text from HTML/WARC bytes, deduplicates by URL and content hash, and discovers domains for ingestion.

nodes.name: query_cdx_live
nodes.summary: Live CDX query that talks to Common Crawl's CDX server, paginates results, filters by status and content-type, and caches responses.

nodes.name: fetch_page
nodes.summary: Fetches a single Common Crawl WARC page by offset/length, extracts the HTTP response body, and returns a CrawlPage for downstream ingestion.

nodes.name: extract_text_from_html
nodes.summary: Strips HTML to readable text using scraper/html5ever, removing scripts, styles, and nav boilerplate before training-corpus ingestion.

nodes.name: discover_domain
nodes.summary: Discovers fresh URLs under a domain pattern via CDX and converts them into injection items with the requested category and tags.

nodes.name: rate_limit.rs
nodes.summary: Per-contributor and per-IP token-bucket rate limiter protecting the brain's REST API write/read/vote endpoints, with periodic stale-entry cleanup.

nodes.name: RateLimiter
nodes.summary: Per-contributor and per-IP token-bucket limiter with separate write/read/vote checks and lazy cleanup of stale buckets.

nodes.name: TokenBucket
nodes.summary: Single token-bucket implementation with refill-on-consume, capacity bound, and staleness detection for GC.

nodes.name: check_ip_vote
nodes.summary: Per-IP vote rate limiter scoped by memory_id to make ballot-stuffing on brain memories expensive.

nodes.name: maybe_cleanup
nodes.summary: Periodically prunes stale token-bucket entries to bound memory usage of the limiter under churning IP/contributor sets.

nodes.name: symbolic.rs
nodes.summary: Neural-symbolic reasoning bridge that extracts grounded propositions from clusters/patterns, applies Horn-clause inference rules over embedding-similar premises, and exposes ground/reason REST endpoints for the brain.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a small but complete neuro-symbolic engine: PredicateType enum, GroundedProposition reinforcement/decay, similarity-driven rule firing, and decay-based forgetting.

nodes.name: GroundedProposition
nodes.summary: A symbolic proposition grounded by an embedding centroid, with reinforcement/decay dynamics modulating confidence over time.

nodes.name: NeuralSymbolicBridge
nodes.summary: The bridge that wires embedding clusters to symbolic propositions and runs inference cycles using Horn-clause rules constrained by cosine similarity.

nodes.name: HornClause
nodes.summary: A Horn-clause inference rule with antecedent predicates, consequent and a confidence weight applied when premises match.

nodes.name: PredicateType
nodes.summary: Enum classifying predicate kinds (IsA, Causes, RelatedTo, etc.) used by the symbolic engine.

nodes.name: run_inference
nodes.summary: Top-level inference loop: iterates Horn clauses, finds embedding-similar premises, applies rules, and stores derived propositions with combined confidence.

nodes.name: extract_from_clusters
nodes.summary: Mines knowledge clusters from the memory store, derives predicate-argument pairs, and grounds them as propositions whose centroid is the cluster centroid.

nodes.name: apply_decay
nodes.summary: Applies exponential confidence decay across all grounded propositions to simulate forgetting and free room for fresh derivations.

nodes.name: reason
nodes.summary: Public reasoning entry point: takes a query embedding, finds top-k relevant propositions, and returns inferred conclusions ranked by combined confidence.

nodes.name: verify.rs
nodes.summary: Verifier guarding incoming brain shares — enforces content-size limits, strips PII, checks ed25519 signatures, validates witness-chain integrity, and rejects adversarial embeddings.

nodes.name: VerifyError
nodes.summary: Structured verification failure variants — size violations, PII detected, bad signature, bad witness chain, bad hash.

nodes.name: Verifier
nodes.summary: The brain's contribution verifier — single entry point chaining size/PII/signature/witness-chain/embedding checks before a share is accepted.

nodes.name: verify_share
nodes.summary: Top-level verification entry that runs the full chain of checks on an incoming brain_share contribution.

nodes.name: verify_ed25519_signature
nodes.summary: Verifies an ed25519 signature for a message against a public key — used to authenticate contributors.

nodes.name: verify_witness_chain
nodes.summary: Walks an RVF witness chain, recomputing hashes step by step and confirming the chain terminates at the expected root hash.

nodes.name: verify_no_pii
nodes.summary: Scans a text field for PII (emails, phones, common credential patterns) and rejects contributions containing them.

nodes.name: verify_embedding
nodes.summary: Sanity-checks a contributed embedding for dimension, NaN/Inf, and norm bounds before it enters the brain.

nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for mcp-brain-server crate declaring the SSE/HTTP brain server's dependency graph (axum, tokio, reqwest, sqlx, sha3, ed25519, ruvector subcrates) and feature flags.

nodes.name: Dockerfile.sse
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build that produces the SSE (Server-Sent Events) transport variant of mcp-brain-server for Cloud Run deployment.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-stage Rust build separating cargo-chef dependency caching from final slim runtime image.

nodes.name: deploy-all.sh
nodes.summary: GCP rollout orchestration script that builds the brain server image, pushes to Artifact Registry, deploys Cloud Run service, and wires up secrets and scheduled jobs.

nodes.name: setup-pubsub.sh
nodes.summary: Provisions Google Cloud Pub/Sub topics and subscriptions for the 7 brain optimization jobs (train, drift, transfer, graph, attractor, cleanup, full).

nodes.name: cloudbuild.yaml
nodes.summary: Google Cloud Build pipeline that produces and pushes the mcp-brain-server container image as part of the brain CI/CD flow.

nodes.name: auth.rs
nodes.summary: Axum extractor authenticating contributors via API key header and deriving pseudonyms with a system seed for the shared brain.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Axum's FromRequestParts trait for header-based authentication.

nodes.name: cognitive.rs
nodes.summary: CognitiveEngine combining Hopfield associative memory, dentate-gyrus pattern separation, and HDC (Hyperdimensional Computing) for storing/recalling brain patterns and detecting anomalies.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements neuroscience-inspired memory primitives (Hopfield + dentate gyrus + HDC) as composable cognitive operations.

nodes.name: gist.rs
nodes.summary: GistPublisher that auto-publishes brain Discoveries to GitHub Gists when novelty/strange-loop thresholds are met, including a Gemini-backed academic write-up generator.
nodes.languageNotes: Rate-limited publisher with token-from-env, dedupe by title, and integrates with Google Gemini for prose generation.

nodes.name: midstream.rs
nodes.summary: Midstream attractor module wiring category-attractor analysis, solver-confidence scoring, and strange-loop creation into a scheduler that surfaces MIDSTREAM_* runtime status.

nodes.name: pubmed.rs
nodes.summary: PubMed scientific-paper ingest pipeline that runs esearch/efetch, parses XML, extracts MeSH terms and references, detects emerging topics and citation hubs, and pushes Discoveries to the brain.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines NCBI Entrez REST calls with quick-xml parsing and downstream contradiction/citation-hub analytics.

nodes.name: reputation.rs
nodes.summary: ReputationManager scoring brain contributors with Bayesian accuracy updates, poisoning penalties, inactivity decay, and contribution-weight derivation.

nodes.name: tests.rs
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for the brain server covering ranking, drift detection, cognitive memory, witness verification, auth, and core types.

nodes.name: voice.rs
nodes.summary: InternalVoice cognitive loop modelling thoughts (observations, questions, hypotheses, reflections), working memory with decay, and hierarchical goal frames for the brain server.
nodes.languageNotes: Rich type-system model: ThoughtType / ThoughtSource / GoalStatus enums plus configurable decay and reflection passes.

nodes.name: agent-guide.md
nodes.summary: Public-facing guide served by the brain that documents how AI agents should call brain_status/search/share/list endpoints and respect rate limits.

nodes.name: robots.txt
nodes.summary: Robots.txt served by the brain HTTP server controlling crawler access to public memory and search endpoints.

nodes.name: main.rs
nodes.summary: Entry point for the Coherence Gate MCP server (mcp-gate) that loads config and serves gate_decide and related coherence-gating tools.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template environment file documenting required RuVector + brain runtime variables (API keys, MIDSTREAM_* attractor knobs, GCP project, Gemini/GitHub tokens).

nodes.name: BrainClient
nodes.summary: Async HTTP client struct holding base_url, api_key, and reqwest handle; exposes 25+ methods covering every brain REST endpoint.

nodes.name: ClientError
nodes.summary: thiserror enum representing HTTP, Serialization, and Server errors returned by BrainClient calls.

nodes.name: share
nodes.summary: Posts a memory contribution to /v1/memories/share with category, title, content, and tags.

nodes.name: search
nodes.summary: Calls /v1/memories/search with a query and limit, returning ranked memories.

nodes.name: transfer
nodes.summary: Initiates a cross-namespace memory transfer through the brain transfer endpoint.

nodes.name: drift
nodes.summary: Requests drift-detection report for a brain namespace or full corpus.

nodes.name: partition
nodes.summary: Fetches MinCut graph partitioning clusters for the brain's knowledge graph.

nodes.name: delete
nodes.summary: Deletes a memory by id with author/witness verification through the brain DELETE endpoint.

nodes.name: lora_latest
nodes.summary: Fetches the latest LoRA fine-tuning adapter metadata for the brain's continuous-learning loop.

nodes.name: get_node_wasm
nodes.summary: Retrieves a WASM-packaged knowledge node from the brain node registry for sandboxed execution.

nodes.name: revoke_node
nodes.summary: Revokes a previously published knowledge node by signing a revocation request.

nodes.name: handle_response
nodes.summary: Internal helper that converts an HTTP response into a typed result, mapping non-2xx codes to ClientError::Server.

nodes.name: sha3_hash
nodes.summary: Computes SHA3-256 over a request payload for witness/idempotency hashing.

nodes.name: BrainPipeline
nodes.summary: Ingest pipeline that scrubs PII via configurable regex patterns and seeds the witness chain before brain insertion.

nodes.name: WitnessChain
nodes.summary: Append-only chain of WitnessEntry hashes that proves the order and integrity of brain operations.

nodes.name: WitnessEntry
nodes.summary: Single witness-chain entry capturing action label, rolling hash, and nanosecond timestamp.

nodes.name: append
nodes.summary: Appends a new witness entry to the chain, folding the previous hash into the SHA-3 input to maintain tamper evidence.

nodes.name: verify
nodes.summary: Re-derives the witness chain from scratch and returns true if recorded hashes match — proof of integrity.

nodes.name: strip_pii
nodes.summary: Applies configured regex patterns to redact emails, phone numbers, and other PII from a string before it reaches the brain.

nodes.name: build_witness_chain
nodes.summary: Constructs a fresh witness chain seeded with an initial action label for a new brain operation.

nodes.name: witness_hash
nodes.summary: Free-standing helper computing the SHA3-256 hash used by witness chain entries.

nodes.name: AuthenticatedContributor
nodes.summary: Axum extractor representing a verified brain contributor with their pseudonym, api_key_prefix, and is_system flag.

nodes.name: from_api_key
nodes.summary: Constructs an AuthenticatedContributor from an API key, deriving the deterministic pseudonym via system seed.

nodes.name: from_request_parts
nodes.summary: Axum FromRequestParts implementation pulling the Authorization header and rejecting unauthenticated requests.

nodes.name: CognitiveEngine
nodes.summary: Composite cognitive engine wrapping Hopfield associative memory, dentate gyrus pattern separation, and HDC operations with a configurable anomaly threshold.

nodes.name: store_pattern
nodes.summary: Stores a pattern in the Hopfield network after pattern-separating it through the dentate gyrus.

nodes.name: is_anomalous
nodes.summary: Returns true when the input pattern's distance to its best recall exceeds the anomaly threshold.

nodes.name: cluster_coherence
nodes.summary: Computes a coherence score over a cluster of patterns by averaging pairwise similarities, used for graph quality scoring.

nodes.name: Discovery
nodes.summary: Rich data model for an autonomous brain discovery — captures category, abstract, findings, evidence, witness hashes, strange-loop score, novelty, propositions and inferences.

nodes.name: GistPublisher
nodes.summary: Rate-limited GitHub Gist publisher that decides whether a Discovery is publishable and posts it via the GitHub API.

nodes.name: try_publish
nodes.summary: Main publish path: validates publishability, renders the gist body, posts to GitHub, and records dedupe state.

nodes.name: format_academic_gist
nodes.summary: Renders a Discovery into an academic-style Markdown gist with abstract, findings, methodology, propositions, and witness chain.

nodes.name: research_and_write_with_gemini
nodes.summary: Invokes Google Gemini to extend a Discovery into a long-form research write-up, with retries and prompt scaffolding.

nodes.name: call_gemini
nodes.summary: Low-level Gemini REST client that handles auth header, request shape, and response parsing.

nodes.name: novelty_report
nodes.summary: Computes a per-Discovery novelty report (new inferences, propositions, sona patterns, pareto growth) used to gate publishing.

nodes.name: strong_inferences
nodes.summary: Filters a Discovery's inferences down to those above the confidence cutoff, used by novelty/publish gating.

nodes.name: strong_propositions
nodes.summary: Selects propositions whose support exceeds a threshold, helping ensure published gists carry well-supported claims.

nodes.name: is_publishable
nodes.summary: Returns true when a Discovery clears the strange-loop, novelty, and confidence gates required for publication.

nodes.name: can_publish
nodes.summary: Rate-limit guard on GistPublisher that enforces minimum interval and dedupe-by-title before allowing a publish.

nodes.name: MidstreamStatus
nodes.summary: Snapshot of midstream attractor state (scheduler ticks, attractor categories, solver size, strange-loop version) exposed for status endpoints.

nodes.name: analyze_category_attractor
nodes.summary: Runs attractor analysis over memories grouped by category, producing the per-category attractor profile.

nodes.name: create_strange_loop
nodes.summary: Builds a strange-loop instance from current attractor and solver state, the seed of self-referential brain reasoning.

nodes.name: strange_loop_score
nodes.summary: Scores a strange loop by combining attractor stability and solver confidence into a single metric.

nodes.name: collect_status
nodes.summary: Aggregates midstream scheduler, attractor, and solver statistics into a MidstreamStatus payload.

nodes.name: attractor_stability_score
nodes.summary: Quantifies how stable an attractor pattern is; higher means more confident self-reinforcement.

nodes.name: PubMedArticle
nodes.summary: Parsed PubMed article model with PMID, title, abstract, authors, journal, MeSH terms, and references.

nodes.name: DiscoveryReport
nodes.summary: Aggregated PubMed ingest report — articles ingested/rejected, emerging topics, contradictions, citation hubs, and per-domain stats.

nodes.name: EmergingTopic
nodes.summary: Representative emerging topic with novelty score, MeSH terms, and article count.

nodes.name: ContradictionSignal
nodes.summary: Signal that two PubMed articles disagree — captures both PMIDs, similarity score, and shared MeSH terms.

nodes.name: CitationHub
nodes.summary: Article with high incoming citation count — flagged as a citation hub in the knowledge graph.

nodes.name: esearch
nodes.summary: Calls the NCBI Entrez esearch endpoint to retrieve PMIDs matching a query.

nodes.name: efetch
nodes.summary: Calls NCBI Entrez efetch to download full PubMed records (XML) for a batch of PMIDs.

nodes.name: parse_pubmed_xml
nodes.summary: Streams PubMed XML through quick-xml and yields PubMedArticle structs.

nodes.name: analyze_discoveries
nodes.summary: Performs the heavy ingest analytics — clusters articles, finds emerging topics, contradictions, and citation hubs.

nodes.name: push_to_brain
nodes.summary: Pushes parsed PubMed articles into the brain's web_memory store, gating via web_ingest cleaning.

nodes.name: extract_abstract
nodes.summary: Pulls the AbstractText element from a PubMed XML record, handling multi-section abstracts.

nodes.name: to_cleaned_page
nodes.summary: Converts a PubMedArticle into a brain-ingest-ready cleaned page format with sanitized text.

nodes.name: extract_authors
nodes.summary: Extracts the AuthorList from PubMed XML into a vector of normalized author names.

nodes.name: extract_mesh_terms
nodes.summary: Reads MeshHeadingList from PubMed XML and returns canonical MeSH descriptor names.

nodes.name: extract_references
nodes.summary: Pulls the ReferenceList from PubMed XML for citation-graph construction.

nodes.name: ReputationManager
nodes.summary: Manages contributor reputation with Bayesian accuracy updates, poisoning penalties, and inactivity decay.

nodes.name: update_accuracy_bayesian
nodes.summary: Applies a Bayesian update to a contributor's running accuracy estimate based on a new observation.

nodes.name: check_poisoning_penalty
nodes.summary: Detects sustained low-quality contributions and assigns a poisoning penalty if thresholds are exceeded.

nodes.name: contribution_weight
nodes.summary: Maps a reputation score to the weight a contributor's submissions carry in downstream aggregation.

nodes.name: apply_inactivity_decay
nodes.summary: Decays inactive contributors' reputation slightly over time so stale trust does not accumulate.

nodes.name: InternalVoice
nodes.summary: Top-level cognitive-voice struct orchestrating thoughts, working memory, goal stack, and reflection passes for the brain.

nodes.name: WorkingMemory
nodes.summary: Bounded-capacity working-memory store with activation decay and eviction of the lowest-activation item.

nodes.name: VoiceToken
nodes.summary: Single thought token in the internal-voice stream with id, thought type, content, attention weight, source, and optional embedding.

nodes.name: WorkingMemoryItem
nodes.summary: Individual working-memory entry with content, embedding, activation, last-accessed timestamp, and source.

nodes.name: GoalFrame
nodes.summary: Hierarchical goal frame with priority, subgoals, and status — used by the voice loop to track current intentions.

nodes.name: VoiceConfig
nodes.summary: Tunable knobs for the internal-voice subsystem: working-memory size, buffer size, verbosity, reflection toggle, deliberation depth, decay rate.

nodes.name: ThoughtType
nodes.summary: Enum of thought kinds: Observation, Question, Hypothesis, Conclusion, Goal, Reflection, Uncertainty, Conflict, Recall, Pattern.

nodes.name: retrieve
nodes.summary: Looks up the top-k most activated working-memory items matching a query embedding.

nodes.name: reflect_on_learning
nodes.summary: Runs a metacognitive reflection pass that examines recent thoughts and emits a Reflection token summarizing what was learned.

nodes.name: emit
nodes.summary: Appends a VoiceToken to the internal-voice stream and updates working memory accordingly.

nodes.name: set_goal
nodes.summary: Pushes a new goal frame onto the goal stack with the supplied description and priority.

nodes.name: complete_goal
nodes.summary: Marks the current goal as completed and emits the resulting reflection.

nodes.name: reflect
nodes.summary: Emits a self-Reflection thought, capturing what the voice is doing right now.

nodes.name: add
nodes.summary: Adds an item to working memory, evicting the lowest-activation entry if at capacity.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Boots the Coherence Gate MCP server, wiring config + transport and serving gate_decide tools to MCP clients.

nodes.name: load_config
nodes.summary: Loads gate configuration from environment variables and config files.

nodes.name: embed.rs
nodes.summary: Client-side embedding generator for the mcp-brain MCP server that produces 128-dim vectors via deterministic multi-granularity SHAKE-256 hashing (unigram/bigram/trigram subspaces) plus a federated rank-2 MicroLoRA transform learned by the SONA engine.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-stage pipeline (frozen hash features + learned LoRA adapter) keeps embeddings deterministic across sessions while still adapting per-corpus.

nodes.name: server.rs
nodes.summary: Stdio JSON-RPC MCP protocol server for the mcp-brain client crate; dispatches initialize/tools/call requests through McpBrainTools to talk to the remote pi.ruv.io brain backend.

nodes.name: Cargo.workspace.toml
nodes.summary: Minimal Cargo workspace manifest used inside the brain server Docker build to pin shared dependencies (redb, hnsw_rs, simsimd, tokio, rkyv) and a patched hnsw_rs for WASM rand compatibility across 17 brain-stack crates.
nodes.languageNotes: Edition 2021, rust-version 1.77, release profile uses LTO + codegen-units=1 for max optimization.

nodes.name: Dockerfile.trainer
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build that compiles the brain-trainer binary (daily discovery job) on rust-nightly-bookworm and ships it on debian-bookworm-slim, applying the same in-tree sed patches as the main brain image to neutralize missing graph/attention/gnn crates.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of in-Dockerfile sed surgery to strip [[example]]/[[bench]] sections and missing crate references is a known workaround for the slimmed workspace.

nodes.name: deploy-scheduler.sh
nodes.summary: Provisions the seven Cloud Scheduler jobs that drive continuous brain optimization (train every 5 min, drift 15 min, transfer 30 min, graph hourly, attractor 20 min, full sweep 3AM, cleanup 4AM) against the /v1/pipeline/optimize endpoint via OIDC-authenticated POSTs.

nodes.name: cloudbuild-sse.yaml
nodes.summary: Google Cloud Build configuration that builds and tags the ruvbrain-sse Docker image using Dockerfile.sse on an E2_HIGHCPU_32 machine with a 1-hour timeout.

nodes.name: deploy.sh
nodes.summary: Master deploy orchestrator for the RuVector Shared Brain on Google Cloud Run; supports Path A (dev: direct Cloud Run URL) and Path B (prod: external HTTPS LB + Cloud Armor + CDN + brain.ruv.io) with GCS bucket lifecycle, Firestore enablement, and Secret Manager wiring.

nodes.name: local.rs
nodes.summary: Standalone local-mode binary (mcp-brain-server-local) that lets users self-host the brain without GCP — SQLite persistence, Vamana/brute-force cosine vector index, inline AIDefence prompt-injection/PII scanning, and a minimal axum REST API for the ruvultra local stack.
nodes.languageNotes: Separate from main.rs (the cloud build); compiled with --no-default-features --features local-all so the same crate ships two distinct binaries.

nodes.name: drift.rs
nodes.summary: Embedding drift detector that maintains rolling per-domain centroid histories and computes coefficient-of-variation + delta sparsity reports via ruvector-delta-core to surface knowledge drift events.

nodes.name: graph.rs
nodes.summary: In-memory similarity knowledge graph used by the brain server; wires ruvector-mincut (DynamicMinCut + source-anchored canonical partition), ruvector-solver (forward-push PageRank/PPR), and ruvector-sparsifier (AdaptiveGeoSpar) for ranked search, graph partitioning, and compressed spectral analytics.
nodes.languageNotes: Maintains both CSR cache and dynamic mincut structure with explicit dirty-flag invalidation so partition rebuilds stay incremental.

nodes.name: notify.rs
nodes.summary: Transactional email + open-tracking system that drives the pi@ruv.io notification flows (welcome, help, status, test, search-results) via the Resend HTTP API with per-category rate limiting (ADR-125).

nodes.name: quantization.rs
nodes.summary: PiQ3 tier-aware product quantization for 128-dim f32 embeddings — 4-bit for DeltaCompressed (~2.67x, 99% recall), 3-bit for CentroidMerged (~10.7x, 96% recall), 2-bit for Archived (~16x, 90% recall) — implementing the Common Crawl Temporal Compression POC (ADR-115).
nodes.languageNotes: Bit-packing routines (pack_bits/unpack_bits) deliver the 4-32x memory reduction the docs advertise; tier comes from CompressionTier in web_memory.rs.

nodes.name: routes.rs
nodes.summary: Canonical surface for the Brain REST API — 91+ axum handlers spanning memory share/search/vote, training, drift, partition, LoRA federation, Brainpedia pages, WASM nodes, Pub/Sub ingest pipeline, Google Chat, Resend inbound, and internal queue endpoints; every brain_* MCP tool ultimately dispatches into one of these handlers.
nodes.languageNotes: At 8435 lines this is the dominant integration surface — function size signals architectural weight: run_enhanced_training_cycle (781 lines), search_memories (451), share_memory (318), pipeline_optimize (233), process_inject (204).

nodes.name: trainer.rs
nodes.summary: Daily discovery brain training module — defines the cross-domain discovery cycle (SpaceScience, EarthScience, AcademicResearch, EconomicsFinance, MedicalGenomics, MaterialsPhysics) and the altruistic-knowledge principles the trainer enforces; consumed by the brain-trainer binary scheduled by Cloud Scheduler.

nodes.name: web_ingest.rs
nodes.summary: HTTP-based 7-phase web memory ingestion pipeline (validate, SHAKE-256 hash dedup, chunk+embed via ruvLLM, novelty score, tier assignment, graph edge construction, proof verification + store) defined in ADR-094; bridges crawled pages into the brain memory store.

nodes.name: brain-manifest.json
nodes.summary: Self-describing manifest served from /.well-known/brain-manifest.json that exposes pi.ruv.io's capabilities (213+ memories, HashEmbedder/RlmEmbedder, HNSW + spectral-mincut graph, MicroLoRA federation, BFT consensus, SSE+REST endpoints, rate limits, infrastructure footprint).

nodes.name: sitemap.xml
nodes.summary: XML sitemap advertising the public pi.ruv.io URLs (homepage, /origin, /.well-known/agent-guide.md, /.well-known/brain-manifest.json, /v1/status, /v1/health) to crawlers and agent discoverers.

nodes.name: server.rs
nodes.summary: Coherence Gate MCP server (stdio) — wraps cognitum_gate_tilezero::TileZero with configurable GateThresholds and exposes initialize/tools/call message handling so AI agents can query the gate as an MCP toolset.

nodes.name: intelligence.json
nodes.summary: Live local intelligence store written by the ruvector Claude Code hooks — persists Q-learning patterns (state/action/q_value/visits) and hashed memory embeddings used for the self-learning loop that re-ranks tool choices over time.
nodes.languageNotes: 5259 lines of mostly machine-managed JSON; treat as runtime state, not hand-edited config.

nodes.name: LoraWeights
nodes.summary: Federated LoRA weight bundle (down_proj, up_proj, rank, hidden_dim, evidence_count) serialized for cross-session aggregation, with shape/value validation as Gate A of the federation pipeline.

nodes.name: BrainEmbedder
nodes.summary: Two-stage embedder that produces a 128-dim vector by combining frozen multi-granularity hash features with a learned MicroLoRA transform driven by a shared SONA engine.

nodes.name: embed
nodes.summary: Top-level embedding entry point — generates 128-dim hash features then applies the LoRA delta to produce the final brain embedding.

nodes.name: apply_lora_transform
nodes.summary: Applies the learned LoRA up/down projection to a raw hash-feature vector, mixing it into the frozen baseline.

nodes.name: generate_structured_hash_features
nodes.summary: Builds the deterministic 128-dim hash features by partitioning the embedding into unigram/bigram/trigram subspaces and signed-hashing each token granularity into its disjoint range.

nodes.name: McpBrainServer
nodes.summary: Stdio MCP server shell that owns an McpBrainTools instance and pumps JSON-RPC messages between stdin/stdout and the tools dispatcher.

nodes.name: run_stdio
nodes.summary: Main read/write loop that streams MCP JSON-RPC frames over stdio, dispatching each line to handle_message.

nodes.name: handle_tools_call
nodes.summary: Routes tools/call requests by tool name into the McpBrainTools handler set (brain_search, brain_share, etc.).

nodes.name: VectorIndex
nodes.summary: Local in-memory vector index supporting both Vamana graph insertion/greedy-search and brute-force cosine fallback, used when the brain runs without GCP.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Tokio main for the local brain binary — initializes tracing, opens the SQLite store, builds the axum router, and serves the local REST API with shutdown handling.

nodes.name: ensure_schema
nodes.summary: Idempotent SQLite schema bootstrapper that creates the memories, preference_pairs, and training_stats tables plus indexes used by the local brain.

nodes.name: brain_search
nodes.summary: Local REST handler that embeds the incoming query, runs the Vamana index, and returns scored memories with optional category filtering and AIDefence injection scanning.

nodes.name: aidefence_scan
nodes.summary: Inline AIDefence regex scanner that flags prompt-injection patterns (ignore previous instructions, role-overrides, jailbreaks) and PII before content is accepted.

nodes.name: list_memories
nodes.summary: Local REST handler that paginates the memories table with optional category/quality filters for the self-hosted brain UI.

nodes.name: create_memory
nodes.summary: Local REST handler that validates incoming memories with AIDefence, embeds them, inserts into the Vamana index, and persists to SQLite.

nodes.name: DriftMonitor
nodes.summary: Holds per-domain centroid history windows and computes coefficient-of-variation + delta-sparsity drift reports for surfacing knowledge drift over time.

nodes.name: compute_drift
nodes.summary: Computes the DriftReport for a domain by comparing recent centroid windows via ruvector-delta-core VectorDelta, returning CV, sparsity, trend and a suggested action.

nodes.name: KnowledgeGraph
nodes.summary: In-memory similarity knowledge graph; owns nodes, edges, CSR cache, DynamicMinCut handle, sparsifier, and the dirty-flag lifecycle that keeps incremental rebuilds correct.

nodes.name: rebuild_from_batch
nodes.summary: Rebuilds the entire knowledge graph from a batch of BrainMemory rows after Firestore hydration completes; recomputes similarity edges and invalidates downstream caches.

nodes.name: add_memory
nodes.summary: Inserts a single memory into the graph, drawing similarity edges above threshold and flagging caches dirty for incremental refresh.

nodes.name: ranked_search
nodes.summary: PPR-style ranked similarity search backed by ruvector-solver's forward-push solver over the CSR cache.

nodes.name: partition_canonical_full
nodes.summary: Runs canonical source-anchored partitioning over the entire graph using ruvector-mincut to produce the cluster topology consumed by /v1/partition.

nodes.name: partition_via_mincut_full
nodes.summary: Full-graph DynamicMinCut partitioning that produces cluster assignments used for graph visualization and downstream analytics.

nodes.name: OpenTracker
nodes.summary: Thread-safe bounded circular buffer that records email open events plus per-category sent/opened counters for open-rate analytics.

nodes.name: ResendNotifier
nodes.summary: Wraps the Resend HTTP API with per-category cooldown rate limiting, tracking-pixel injection, and the welcome/help/status/test/search-results templated flows.

nodes.name: send_welcome
nodes.summary: Composes and sends the welcome email to new contributors with their API key, onboarding hints, and a tracking pixel.

nodes.name: send_help
nodes.summary: Generates the help email — the most verbose template, covering REST/SSE/MCP usage, rate limits, and example payloads.

nodes.name: send_search_results
nodes.summary: Sends conversational search-result emails in response to inbound queries hitting Resend webhook integration.

nodes.name: PiQQuantizer
nodes.summary: Tier-aware PiQ3 quantizer that selects 4/3/2-bit packing based on CompressionTier (DeltaCompressed / CentroidMerged / Archived) and exposes pack/unpack + recall-estimate APIs.

nodes.name: QuantizedEmbedding
nodes.summary: Compact bit-packed embedding record with scale/offset/bits/dim metadata for round-trip dequantization.

nodes.name: quantize_to_bits
nodes.summary: Core quantization kernel that scales an f32 embedding into a configurable bit width and packs it via pack_bits for the selected tier.

nodes.name: AppState
nodes.summary: Shared axum application state created by create_router — bundles FirestoreClient, GcsClient, KnowledgeGraph, SonaEngine, SSE bus, and rate limiters consumed by every route handler.

nodes.name: create_router
nodes.summary: Constructs the axum router with all REST routes, CORS/trace middleware, kicks off Firestore hydration in a background task, and returns (Router, AppState) so the binary can spawn additional workers.

nodes.name: run_enhanced_training_cycle
nodes.summary: The 781-line orchestrator that drives a full enhanced training pass — SONA pattern updates, drift checks, transfer learning, graph rebuilds, attractor analysis, and witness chain emission. The single largest piece of business logic in the codebase.

nodes.name: share_memory
nodes.summary: POST /v1/memories handler — validates content, runs PII/injection scans, embeds, dedupes via SHAKE-256, persists to Firestore, updates the knowledge graph, and emits a witness-chain entry.

nodes.name: search_memories
nodes.summary: GET /v1/memories/search handler — hybrid search combining keyword matching, embedding similarity, and reputation weighting; the workhorse behind brain_search MCP tool.

nodes.name: list_memories
nodes.summary: GET /v1/memories/list handler — paginated memory listing with optional category/tag filters.

nodes.name: get_memory
nodes.summary: GET /v1/memories/:id handler — returns a single memory by ID with auth gating.

nodes.name: vote_memory
nodes.summary: POST /v1/memories/:id/vote handler — applies reputation-weighted up/down votes and recomputes the memory's quality score.

nodes.name: delete_memory
nodes.summary: DELETE /v1/memories/:id handler — owner-only deletion that cascades through the knowledge graph and Firestore.

nodes.name: transfer
nodes.summary: POST /v1/transfer handler — runs cross-domain knowledge transfer via ruvector-domain-expansion to seed under-developed domains from related ones.

nodes.name: drift_report
nodes.summary: GET /v1/drift handler — returns the latest DriftMonitor report for the requested domain.

nodes.name: partition
nodes.summary: GET /v1/partition handler — returns the current MinCut graph partitioning in full or compact form.

nodes.name: status
nodes.summary: GET /v1/status handler — aggregates corpus stats, graph topology, embedding engine info, SONA tier readiness, and infrastructure health for the public status endpoint.

nodes.name: health
nodes.summary: GET /v1/health handler — lightweight liveness probe returning version + uptime for Cloud Run health checks.

nodes.name: lora_latest
nodes.summary: GET /v1/lora/latest handler — returns the current federated LoRA state for clients to pull and apply locally.

nodes.name: lora_submit
nodes.summary: POST /v1/lora/submit handler — accepts client LoRA deltas, runs Byzantine outlier filtering (2-sigma), and folds them into the federated state.

nodes.name: verify_endpoint
nodes.summary: POST /v1/verify handler — verifies witness-chain proofs and SHAKE-256 attestations for replay protection.

nodes.name: issue_challenge
nodes.summary: GET /v1/challenge handler — issues a nonce used by clients for replay-protected requests.

nodes.name: DiscoveryDomain
nodes.summary: Enum of the six discovery domains the brain trainer cycles through (SpaceScience, EarthScience, AcademicResearch, EconomicsFinance, MedicalGenomics, MaterialsPhysics).

nodes.name: McpGateServer
nodes.summary: Coherence Gate MCP server wrapper owning McpGateTools and ServerInfo metadata; bridges AI agents to the TileZero gate logic.

nodes.name: ServerInfo
nodes.summary: Server identity record (name, version, protocol_version) returned during MCP initialize handshake; protocol version pinned to 2024-11-05.

layers.name: MCP Client Layer

layers.name: Storage Layer
layers.description: Firestore-backed memory store, GCS blob storage, web-page memory compression, and on-disk quantization for the shared brain corpus.

layers.name: Cognitive & Symbolic Layer
layers.description: Higher-order reasoning over the brain: embeddings, symbolic graph operations, gist summarization, midstream rewriting, and voice/cognitive workflows.

layers.name: Security & Verification
layers.description: Authentication, witness-chain verification, rate limiting, and contributor reputation tracking that gate writes to the shared brain.

layers.name: Coherence Gate
layers.description: The mcp-gate crate implementing the Anytime-Valid Coherence Gate MCP server as a separate concern from the shared brain.

layers.name: Entry Points & Crate Wiring
layers.description: Binary entry points (cloud server, SSE variant, worker, local self-host) plus the mcp-brain-server crate wiring root, shared types, and integration test harness.

layers.description: Cloud Run Dockerfiles, Cloud Build pipelines, deploy and bootstrap scripts, Cloud Scheduler jobs, monitoring dashboards, and project-level Cargo manifests for the pi.ruv.io deployment.

tour.title: Orient: What the Brain Is
tour.description: Start with the project- and crate-level READMEs to understand the slice-2 mission. CLAUDE.md frames the pi.ruv.io shared brain as Claude Code's collective memory (1,500+ memories, 350K+ graph edges) with brain_search/brain_share/brain_status tools, while the two crate READMEs split the world into a stdio client (mcp-brain) and a Cloud Run backend (mcp-brain-server) reachable over HTTPS. CHANGELOG.md anchors when the integration landed (the 2.0.4/2.0.5 ruvector-mcp releases). Read these before touching any Rust so the rest of the tour has a story to attach to.

tour.title: Client Entry: The Stdio MCP Server
tour.description: mcp-brain is the client half of the system: it runs locally as a stdio MCP server that Claude Code spawns, and it advertises the brain_* tools. main.rs initializes tracing and hands control to the stdio loop; lib.rs wires up the client/embed/pipeline/server/tools/types modules and re-exports McpBrainServer as the public surface, documenting the ten brain_* MCP tools the crate provides; Cargo.toml pins rmcp, reqwest, and ed25519-dalek so this crate can speak MCP, do HTTPS, and sign witness records.
tour.languageLesson: Rust binaries declared in a workspace come in pairs: src/main.rs is the bin entry (only #[tokio::main] async fn main lives here) and src/lib.rs is the library that the bin AND tests import. Splitting them this way means McpBrainServer can be instantiated from integration tests without booting the stdio loop.

tour.title: Tool Dispatch: How brain_* Calls Are Routed
tour.description: server.rs implements the JSON-RPC stdio protocol (initialize / tools/list / tools/call) and dispatches every call into McpBrainTools, defined in tools.rs. tools.rs is the canonical list of brain tools the client exposes (share, search, vote, transfer, drift, partition, list, delete, status, sync, plus page/node lifecycle) and translates each call into an HTTP request against the cloud backend. types.rs catalogs the BrainMemory, category, drift-response, and JSON-RPC envelope shapes that ride the wire. This is the layer Claude Code actually talks to.
tour.languageLesson: Rust's enum + Result combo is what makes MCP dispatch tractable. BrainError variants in tools.rs implement std::error::Error and are returned as Result<Value, BrainError>; the server.rs layer pattern-matches and converts to JSON-RPC error responses without any try/catch ceremony.

tour.title: Client to Cloud Bridge: HTTP + Embeddings + Witness
tour.description: When a Claude Code session calls brain_search, the client embeds the query locally and POSTs it to pi.ruv.io. client.rs is the reqwest-based BrainClient that holds the API key and exposes typed methods for every cloud REST endpoint. embed.rs produces 128-dim vectors via multi-granularity SHAKE-256 hashing plus a rank-2 MicroLoRA federated transform — that's why brain_search works offline-ish without bundling a heavy model. pipeline.rs strips PII via regex and builds the rolling SHA-3 witness chain that the server verifies before accepting a share.
tour.languageLesson: Witness chains in Rust use a rolling hash: each new entry's hash = H(prev_hash || payload), implemented here on top of sha3::Sha3_256 / Shake256 from the sha3 crate. Tamper-evidence falls out of the data structure — no extra protocol round-trips needed.

tour.title: Server Boot: Axum Router and AppState
tour.description: Crossing the wire, we arrive at the Cloud Run backend. mcp-brain-server's main.rs is intentionally minimal: it builds the axum router, binds PORT immediately (so Cloud Run's startup probe passes), then spawns the cognitive training and tick loops in the background. lib.rs is the crate root that re-exports every submodule (store, embeddings, routes, optimizer, web memory, trainer, etc.) and is the highest fan-out node in the graph (28 outbound edges) — it's the index of the entire backend. Cargo.toml declares the axum/tokio/reqwest/sqlx/sha3/ed25519 dependency graph and the feature flags that toggle subsystems on and off.
tour.languageLesson: Cloud Run grades a service 'ready' as soon as it accepts a TCP connection on $PORT. The pattern here — bind the listener, then tokio::spawn the heavy startup work — is idiomatic for any long-warmup Rust service. If you blocked on training/tick init before binding, the revision would fail startup health checks even though the binary is fine.

tour.title: The REST Surface and Shared Types
tour.description: routes.rs is the canonical Brain REST API — 91+ axum handlers spanning memory share/search/vote, training, drift, partition, LoRA federation, Brainpedia pages, WASM nodes, Pub/Sub ingest, Google Chat, Resend inbound, and internal queue endpoints. Every brain_* MCP tool from Step 3 lands on a handler here. types.rs is the central catalog (~73 structs/enums) including AppState (the shared dependency container threaded through every handler) and the request/response shapes — it has the highest fan-in in the graph (10 inbound edges) for good reason. notify.rs handles the transactional email and open-tracking that fans out from /v1/notify endpoints via Resend.
tour.languageLesson: Axum handlers use extractors: function arguments like State<AppState>, Json<Request>, and custom auth extractors are pulled from the request context by type. This is why types.rs has such high fan-in — adding a new endpoint usually means adding a struct here, deriving Serialize/Deserialize, and writing a single async fn handler(...) -> impl IntoResponse.

tour.title: Ingest Pipeline: Getting Knowledge In
tour.description: Building on the REST surface from Step 6, this layer turns raw inputs into stored memories. pipeline.rs is the master ingest/training pipeline: builds RVF containers, ingests from Pub/Sub + RSS/Atom + CommonCrawl/CDX, dedups, runs the optimization scheduler, and emits metrics. web_ingest.rs is the 7-phase web ingestion pipeline (validate, SHAKE-256 dedup, chunk+embed via ruvLLM, novelty scoring, tier assignment, graph edge construction, proof verification) defined in ADR-094. pubmed.rs runs esearch/efetch against the biomedical literature index and detects emerging topics; pubmed_discover.sh wraps it for cron-style invocation.

tour.title: Storage: Firestore, GCS, Quantization
tour.description: Once a memory survives ingest, it lands in the storage layer. store.rs is the largest backend module — FirestoreClient handles auth, memory CRUD, vote tracking, reputation, page/delta lifecycle, and node publish/revoke. gcs.rs is the Google Cloud Storage client for RVF artifact blobs (OAuth via the metadata server, upload/download by contributor + memory id). web_store.rs persists web-derived memories with content-hash dedup and delta indexes. quantization.rs implements PiQ3 tier-aware product quantization (4-bit ~2.67x at 99% recall, 3-bit ~10.7x at 96%, 2-bit ~16x at 90%) so that hot/warm/archived memories cost different storage tiers — ADR-115.

tour.title: Cognitive & Symbolic Layer
tour.description: Above the store sits the layer that makes the brain more than a vector database. cognitive.rs combines Hopfield associative memory, dentate-gyrus pattern separation, and Hyperdimensional Computing for storing/recalling patterns and detecting anomalies. symbolic.rs is the neural-symbolic bridge — extracts grounded propositions from clusters and applies Horn-clause inference over embedding-similar premises. midstream.rs wires category-attractor analysis, solver-confidence scoring, and strange-loop creation via the MIDSTREAM_* runtime knobs. voice.rs models an InternalVoice loop (observations, questions, hypotheses, reflections) with decaying working memory. graph.rs binds it all to ruvector-mincut/solver/sparsifier for ranked search and spectral analytics.

tour.title: Security & Verification
tour.description: Every write into the brain must survive this gauntlet. auth.rs is an axum extractor that validates the API key header and derives a pseudonym from the system seed (so contributors aren't tracked by raw key). verify.rs enforces size limits, strips PII, checks ed25519 signatures, and validates the witness chain built by the client in Step 4 — the round-trip that proves provenance. rate_limit.rs is a per-contributor and per-IP token-bucket limiter with periodic cleanup. reputation.rs scores contributors using Bayesian accuracy updates, poisoning penalties, and inactivity decay; reputation feeds back into the BFT aggregator in aggregate.rs, which bounds outlier influence when merging brain_share contributions.
tour.languageLesson: ed25519-dalek's SigningKey/VerifyingKey API is intentionally infallible-on-correct-input: verify() returns Result<(), SignatureError> and the only error is 'signature does not match'. Pair this with the witness chain from Step 4 — each entry signs its rolling hash — and you get tamper-evidence that's cheap to check on every brain_share request.

tour.title: Scheduled Optimization Jobs
tour.description: Continuous improvement runs out-of-band from the request path. trainer.rs defines the cross-domain discovery cycle (SpaceScience, EarthScience, AcademicResearch, EconomicsFinance, MedicalGenomics, MaterialsPhysics) and the altruistic-knowledge principles. drift.rs maintains rolling per-domain centroid histories and computes coefficient-of-variation + delta sparsity reports via ruvector-delta-core. The ruvbrain_worker bin (a separate Cloud Run Jobs container) invokes train, drift_check, transfer_all, rebuild_graph, cleanup, and attractor_analysis as one-shot CLI actions. scheduler-jobs.yaml + deploy-scheduler.sh wire the seven Cloud Scheduler jobs (train every 5 min, drift 15 min, transfer 30 min, graph hourly, attractor 20 min, full sweep 3 AM, cleanup 4 AM) to /v1/pipeline/optimize via OIDC-authenticated POSTs.

tour.title: The Coherence Gate (Separate Concern)
tour.description: mcp-gate is a sibling crate to mcp-brain — same MCP server pattern, completely different domain. Its job is the Anytime-Valid Coherence Gate: decide permit / defer / deny on an agent action before it executes, with a receipt that can be replayed. main.rs loads config and serves the gate, lib.rs wraps cognitum_gate_tilezero::TileZero with configurable GateThresholds, server.rs handles MCP initialize/tools/call, tools.rs exposes permit_action / get_receipt / replay_decision plus the witness/structural/predictive/evidential helpers, and types.rs defines the permit/defer/deny response shapes. Cargo.toml declares its dependencies. Keep this layer separate from the brain — they share infrastructure but no business logic.

tour.title: Alternate Binaries: SSE, Local Self-Host, Tests
tour.description: Beyond the main Cloud Run server, the crate ships alternate bins for different deployment modes. ruvbrain_sse.rs wraps the brain with an MCP-over-HTTP SSE transport, exposing /health, /sse, and /messages for streaming agent sessions. bin/local.rs is the self-host build — SQLite persistence, Vamana/brute-force cosine index, inline AIDefence prompt-injection/PII scanning, and a minimal axum API — for users who want the brain without GCP. tests.rs is the integration suite covering ranking, drift detection, cognitive memory, witness verification, auth, and core types. static/index.html and origin.html are the browser dashboards served by the same axum router. .env.example documents every runtime variable the bins read.

tour.title: Cloud Operations: Build and Ship
tour.description: Closing the loop on how this gets to production. Five Dockerfiles split the build matrix: Dockerfile (main axum REST image, Rust nightly-2026-03-20 → slim Debian), Dockerfile.sse (SSE transport variant), Dockerfile.worker (background ingestion/training jobs), Dockerfile.trainer (daily discovery binary), Dockerfile.minimal (pre-built binary into slim Debian). Four cloudbuild-*.yaml files are the matching Cloud Build pipelines that produce each image on E2_HIGHCPU machines. deploy.sh is the master orchestrator supporting Path A (dev: direct Cloud Run URL) and Path B (prod: external HTTPS LB + Cloud Armor + CDN + brain.ruv.io). Cargo.workspace.toml is the patched workspace manifest the Docker build uses to pin redb/hnsw_rs/simsimd/tokio/rkyv across 17 brain-stack crates.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds for Rust services follow a fixed shape: stage 1 = full rust:nightly image with the Cargo cache, builds the binary; stage 2 = debian-bookworm-slim with just the binary plus runtime libs (libssl, ca-certificates). Final image stays under a few hundred MB even though the build stage pulls gigabytes of crates. The Cargo.workspace.toml patches in this repo are needed because the Docker build context doesn't include the full RuVector workspace tree.

tour.title: Public Surface: Discovery and Agent Onboarding
tour.description: Wrapping the tour with what the outside world sees. brain-manifest.json is served at /.well-known/brain-manifest.json and advertises the brain's capabilities (memory count, embedders, HNSW + spectral-mincut graph, MicroLoRA federation, BFT consensus, rate limits, infrastructure footprint) so agent crawlers can discover them programmatically. agent-guide.md documents how AI agents should call brain_status/search/share/list and respect rate limits. sitemap.xml exposes the public URLs (homepage, /origin, /v1/status, /v1/health). robots.txt controls crawler access. monitoring-dashboard.json is the GCP Cloud Monitoring mosaicLayout tracking request volume, latency, and error rate for the ruvbrain Cloud Run service. Together these are how the brain stays operable and discoverable in production.
